surface.name: Bird Channels API send SMS via HTTPS (AccessKey)
surface.selection_basis: MessageBird/Bird messaging docs require an account, active SMS channel, and AccessKey. Sending SMS documents POST https://api.bird.com/workspaces/{workspaceId}/channels/{channelId}/messages with Authorization AccessKey. Prefer HTTPS over SDKs.

alternatives_considered.name: WhatsApp Business onboarding
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Separate channel with Meta approval waits; SMS is the general programmable first send.

alternatives_considered.name: Dashboard-only send UI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: API send is the developer first-success path documented with AccessKey.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Bird/MessageBird workspace, obtain an AccessKey and SMS channel, and send a first SMS via HTTPS.
assumed_prior_state: No Bird workspace, AccessKey, or SMS channel exists.
A terminal with cURL is available.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: POST to the Channels messages endpoint accepts an SMS body and returns an accepted message object.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: HTTP 202 JSON message with status such as accepted/processing for the SMS send.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Sending SMS messages requires active SMS channel + AccessKey and documents POST /workspaces/{workspaceId}/channels/{channelId}/messages returning 202.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: First developer success is an accepted outbound SMS API call, not channel provisioning alone.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to a Bird/MessageBird account and open a workspace.
primary_path.success_signal: Channels and API credentials are reachable.

primary_path.action: Create or select an active SMS channel and note workspaceId and channelId.
Create an AccessKey and store it securely.
primary_path.success_signal: Key value available for Authorization header.

primary_path.action: POST https://api.bird.com/workspaces/{workspaceId}/channels/{channelId}/messages with AccessKey and SMS text body/receiver.
details: Body type text with text.text; receiver contacts include phonenumber identifier.

friction_gates.description: Active SMS channel required before send.

uncertainties.question: Exact dashboard labels for creating AccessKey vs legacy MessageBird live/test keys after the Bird rebrand.
uncertainties.impact: Credential UI steps may use Bird naming.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: API docs specify AccessKey header; console labels can change with rebrand.
